mnemonics.title: BMW Color Codes
mnemonics.content:
YELLOW: Your Anatomy (Human anatomical, soiled, linen, microbiology)
RED: Rubbish Plastics (Gloves, tubes, catheters, plastics)
WHITE: Wastes Sharps (Needles, syringes, broken glass)
BLUE: Bottles/Bulk Glass (Vials, ampoules, metallic implants)

mnemonics.title: Treatment Methods by Waste Type
mnemonics.content:
HOT: Human anatomical - Incineration/Deep burial
Organics: Microbiological - Autoclaving/Microwaving
Plastics: Red bag waste - Autoclaving/Microwaving
Sharps: White bag - Autoclaving/Chemical/Encapsulation

acronyms.usage: All waste generated from healthcare facilities including diagnosis, treatment, and immunization
Prescribed authority for granting authorization to healthcare facilities at state level
Central oversight body for biomedical waste management in India
International body providing guidelines for biomedical waste management

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a traffic signal: Yellow (stop - infectious danger), Red (caution - plastic hazard), White (warning - sharp danger), Blue (clear - glass safety). Think: Y-R-W-B in alphabetical order = Yellow, Red, White, Blue containers.
Picture a kitchen pressure cooker for autoclaving (steam heat), a microwave oven for microwaving, an incinerator as a furnace for burning, and a chemical tank for chlorination.
Start → Is it human/anatomical? → Yellow. Is it plastic? → Red. Is it sharp? → White. Is it glass? → Blue. Simple decision tree for segregation.

analogies.analogy: Like sorting laundry: Whites (sharps) go in white basket, colored clothes (plastics) in red, delicate (anatomical) in yellow, and jeans (glass) in blue.
Like pressure cooking food to kill bacteria - steam under pressure at high temperature sterilizes the waste completely.
Like cremation - high-temperature burning completely destroys organic waste, leaving only ash.
Like traffic police for environmental rules - SPCB is the state-level enforcer, CPCB is the head office at center.
